text: them. They stared at Ronin and Newton as they walked past . There were several doors along the walls, and two large corridors leading out of the chamber — Armory, Barracks, Stockade in red letters over one, Laboratory, Administration, Infirmary in green over the other . Was that where Lara was, the infirmary? It was his only potential lead. The green line on the floor broke away from the others, turning down that corridor. The soldiers continued past it, along the red line. Beneath the sign that said Stockade . The corridor narrowed, and they proceeded through a series of turns, past more doors and hallways, ever deeper into the facility. Finally, they passed through an open door into a large room lined on one side with small cells . Newton and Ronin were guided into a side-chamber, a long room with a long table. There were people seated at the table, some in plain clothing, some in uniforms. Whatever conversation they’d been involved in stopped . “Have a seat,” said the man at the head of the table, lifting a hand to indicate the open chairs in front of Ronin and Newton. The insignia on his blue jacket marked him as a colonel . Ronin sat as the soldiers took up positions around the perimeter of the room. Seconds of relative silence ticked by. The room’s temperature was gradually climbing, and at least one human wore a sheen of sweat on his face . “I’m Colonel Jack Rodriguez,” the man in blue finally said, “Head of Security in this facility. Given your open association with Warlord, and the fact that you came in through an entrance that hasn’t been discovered in two hundred years, I’m very concerned about the safety of my people .” “The woman we brought, in,” Ronin said, optics focused on Rodriguez, “what is her status ?” “She’s in critical condition. Doctor Cooper and her staff are doing all they can to stabilize her .” “I want to see her .” “That’s not how this works. You represent a potential threat. Until I’m certain that threat is nonexistent , I — ” “Warlord is the one who beat her and left me deactivated. We’re unarmed, and didn’t resist when your men took us into custody. We knew where the entrance was because my companion has used it in the past .” All eyes swung to Newton . “I’ve come and gone through that entryway fifty-seven times,” he said . One of the non-uniformed men rose from his chair, hands flat on the table. He was gray-haired with a lined face. “ Newton ?” Newton turned his head to regard the man. A hushed chorus of voices arose, too many and too varied for Ronin to bother isolating any single one . “William?” The plates around Newton’s mouth lifted in a smile. “In all my years away, I somehow disregarded the effect of time. It’s no wonder I didn’t recognize any faces as we entered. Another bit of foolishness on my part .” “I was a boy when you left.” William gestured to the younger man seated beside him. Their resemblance was immediately apparent, despite their age difference. “My son, Will. He has a child of his own .” “That’s Newton?” Rodriguez asked, stoic face turned toward William . “Yes. He’s no threat to us .” “I can assure you, neither myself nor my companion mean harm to anyone here. We came because Miss Brooks was gravely injured, and I lacked the means to tend her .” “Who are you?” Rodriguez’s eyes shifted to Ronin . “I am…was a dustwalker, most recently out of Cheyenne. Ronin .” “You’re built like a soldier. Right down to your hair. Recall any of that time ?” “Images, occasionally. My memory was damaged in the Black Out. I have no specifics .” “Have you retained any of your combat programming ? “I’ve survived fifty-one thousand, six hundred and forty-eight days in the Dust. Ended a lot of individuals that meant me harm. I don’t know what I’ve retained and what I’ve lost, but it doesn’t seem to have much bearing anymore .” “That’s over one hundred and forty years,” someone said. A hush fell over the group . “We…might be able to restore your memory,” William said. “There are several bots here who’ve gone through the procedure with few complications .” It seemed the logical course for Ronin to follow. To learn where he came from, what he’d done before, who he was. To be whole . But what was he missing now? Knowing his place in a dead world wouldn’t do him any good, wouldn’t help his future. He was whole; Lara had given him purpose, given him meaning. His core programming didn’t matter, never truly had. It had served only as a reason to move forward, as motivation to keep him going, and that need had been filled . “My only concern is Lara,” Ronin said . William smiled and nodded, easing into his chair. “ Very well .” The Colonel leaned forward. “As soon as there’s an update — ” “You trust Newton’s word that I’m not here to cause trouble. Let me be with her. That’s all I want .” Rodriguez frowned, brows falling low . “The work Doctor Cooper and her team have to do is very delicate,” William said . “You’ll get in the way.” Rodriguez met Ronin’s optics and held his gaze. “You want her alive, you give them the space they need to work. In the meantime, I want you to tell us everything you know about Warlord. Numbers, armament, defenses. All of it .” Chapter Twenty- Eight R onin sat motionless in the worn leather chair, fingers digging into its arms. His processors blazed despite his outward stillness. Hours had passed with excruciating slowness. Rodriguez’s questions were brief, to-the-point, and seemingly endless. Throughout, Ronin’s thought-chains returned to Lara . There was a knock at the door . “Enter,” Rodriguez called . A
